pkg/framework/entutils/testutils/ent1/sc.purpose: Minimal Ent schema fixture used to test the entutils package (mixins, TransactingRepo, TxDriver). The single Example1 schema is the source of truth that the sibling ../db generated client is built from.

patterns.name: Standard Ent schema struct
patterns.description: Each entity is a struct embedding ent.Schema and implementing the Mixin/Fields/Indexes/Edges methods, even when some return empty slices.
patterns.example: type Example1 struct { ent.Schema }; func (Example1) Fields() []ent.Field { ... }

patterns.name: Reuse entutils mixins, do not hand-roll timestamps
patterns.description: Timestamp/audit fields come from entutils mixins (TimeMixin here), not from manually-declared created_at/updated_at fields.

patterns.name: String immutable id
patterns.description: Primary id is a String field marked Unique().Immutable() rather than an int autoincrement.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Example1 Ent schema (TimeMixin + string id + example_value_1) used as a fixture for entutils tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing fields requires regenerating ../db via `make generate` (entc.go/generate.go); editing the generated db package directly will be overwritten.

anti_patterns: Adding production domain fields here — this is a test fixture, not a real entity; real schemas live in openmeter/ent/schema.
Manually adding created_at/updated_at fields instead of using entutils.TimeMixin.
Editing the generated ../db client to reflect a schema change instead of running code generation.

decisions.decision: Keep a tiny standalone Ent schema separate from the main openmeter schema set.
decisions.rationale: entutils transaction/mixin helpers need a generated Ent client to exercise TransactingRepo/TxDriver in isolation without depending on the full application schema.

code_examples.scenario: Defining an Ent schema fixture with an entutils mixin
